title: Micro Volume Purification of His-Tagged Proteins with IMAC Ni-Charged Resin
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ve4i","text":"This protocol describes a micro volume purification method for His-tagged proteins using IMAC with Ni-charged resin in a mini spin chromatography column. His-tagged proteins are selective bound to a Ni-charged resin due to the interaction between the polyhistidine tag and Ni²⁺ ions and eluted using imidazole-containing buffers. Desalting is the performed using SEC where salts and imidazole are fractionated, while larger molecules like the target protein are excluded. This results in the isolation of purified His-tagged proteins with a high yield and minimal contaminants, suitable for downstream applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eafd7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bc7ev","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14ur","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Resuspend Profinity™ Ni-charged IMAC Resin Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #1560131 and pipette 200 µL into a Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories
Wash the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories by adding 200 µL of distilled water
Centrifuge the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any collected buffer
Centrifuge the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any water collected

Equilibriate the column by adding 200 µL of Equilibration Buffer Contributed by users to the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories The equilibration buffer is 20 mM sodium phosphate, 300 mM NaCl, and 5 mM imidazole.
Centrifuge the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any buffer collected and cap the column
From the soluble fraction of lysed cells containing the Sample add 600 µL to the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories and gently mix for 00:20:00
Centrifuge the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any flowthrough

Add 600 µL of Wash Buffer Contributed by users to the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories and centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any collected buffer The wash buffer is 20 mM sodium phosphate, 300 mM NaCl and 10 mM imidazole.

Snap the bottom and remove the top of a Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories and allow the packing buffer to drain for 00:05:00 The Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories has a fractionation range of 1000 to 6000 Da, so any remaining salts or small proteins will be fractionated.
Centrifuge the Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard any collected buffer
Add the approx. 100 µL of the collected Sample to the Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories The maximum load volume of the column is 100 µL
Centrifuge the Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories at 1000 x g, °C, 5 minutes and any excluded liquid is the purified Sample
Repeat up to 3 times with the same Micro Bio-Spin® Column with Bio-Gel® P-6 Bio-Rad Laboratories to obtain the desired Sample volume

Add 100 µL Elution Buffer Contributed by users to the Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories and centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes , and COLLECT the flowthrough, containing the Sample The elution buffer is 20 mM sodium phosphate, 300 mM NaCl, 250 mM imidazole, pH 8.0 The Mini Bio-Spin® Chromatography Column Bio-Rad Laboratories can now be disposed of
creator: Syon Schlecht
